summary: Addresses review #6 feedback: adds database mutual-exclusivity defense-in-depth check in validate(), adds webhook go:generate directive, fixes CRD output path syntax, and adds 4 database validation tests with positive+negative coverage.

tests_checklist.item: Edge cases covered: both-set, neither-set, host-only, clusterRef-only
New database tests mirror existing cache test structure
Every defaulting rule (5) has positive+negative tests
Every validation rule (7) has positive+negative tests

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing defense-in-depth pattern from Cache check
Database check uses identical boolean-equality idiom as Cache check
go:generate directives use canonical output:crd:artifacts:config= form
No dead code or commented-out code

security_checklist.item: All kubebuilder-marked fields now have matching runtime defense-in-depth checks
Input validation comprehensive: Replicas, MaxActiveKeys, Cache XOR, Database XOR, Cron, Plugins, PolicyOverrides

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is minimal: 10 lines of validation + 47 lines of tests
Database check placed adjacent to Cache check for consistency
Webhook go:generate directive matches Makefile manifests target

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code (pattern reused, not copied)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Database mutual-exclusivity caught at admission time before reconciler runs
Errors accumulated in field.ErrorList for batch reporting

defensive_checklist.item: All XValidation CEL rules now have matching webhook runtime checks
All kubebuilder:validation:Minimum markers now have matching webhook runtime checks
CEL and Go semantics verified equivalent for Database (has() on pointer/string aligns with nil/empty checks)
